data.body:
Render speed and cost-adjusted throughput metrics in the audit screen using duration data captured in the audit log.

## Requirements

### Effective speed metric
The core insight: a fast agent that fails often is slower than a reliable agent that takes longer per attempt. We need to show this.

Formula: `effective_time = avg_duration / success_rate`

More precisely, accounting for wasted review time on rejections:
`effective_time = avg_step_duration + (rejection_rate × (avg_review_duration + effective_time))`
Solving: `effective_time = (avg_step_duration + rejection_rate × avg_review_duration) / success_rate`

This factors in that a rejected step wastes both the step time AND the review time.

### New table: Speed & Throughput
Per agent per step, show:
- Raw Speed: median duration per attempt
- Success Rate: (already have)
- Attempts/Ship: `1 / success_rate` — average attempts needed to get one accepted
- Effective Speed: duration adjusted for failure rate and review overhead
- n: sample size of timed events

### Chart: Effective speed comparison
Bar chart or similar showing effective speed per agent per step, making it immediately obvious which agent delivers shipped work fastest.

### Agent breakdown table updates
Add speed columns to the existing per-agent breakdown:
- Median duration
- Effective speed
- Speed vs mean (offset, like we do for rate)

### Edge cases
- If no timed events exist yet (fresh deploy or all legacy data), show a message like 'Duration data collecting...'
- If only some events have timing, show n/total to indicate coverage
- Handle steps with 0% success rate gracefully (effective speed = infinity → show '-' or 'N/A')

### Dependencies
- Depends on the duration capture knot being complete (needs durationMs on audit events)
- Can stub with synthetic data for development

### Acceptance criteria
- Speed table renders with real data when available
- Effective speed correctly accounts for both attempt duration and review overhead on failures
- Graceful degradation when timing data is partial or absent
- All quality gates pass (lint, typecheck, test, build)
data.title: Render speed and cost-adjusted throughput metrics in audit screen
